substep.title: Assess Each Precedent
substep.action:
For each candidate precedent surfaced in step 2, assess transfer to OUR situation (grounded in what step 1c established). Record: - Source: file:line / thoughts/ alias / URL - What it is: one line on what the precedent does - What matches: the dimensions on which it resembles our subject - What differs: the dimensions on which our context diverges - Transfer-fit class (factual classification, not a recommendation): - directly-reusable — matches closely; could be lifted or imported with minimal change - adaptable — solves the same problem in a differing context; transferable with the identified changes - informative — does not transfer directly but reveals a useful approach, consideration, or pitfall - cautionary — a precedent of what NOT to do; what went wrong and why
State match/differ with evidence. Do NOT say "we should adopt this" — whether to adopt is the orchestrator's decision; the class grades the evidence, it does not prescribe an action.

substep.title: Note Scout Follow-Ups
substep.action: For any external precedent whose value would require reading its code in depth (not just its docs), note a recommended Scout follow-up: the repo and what to study. This is a recommendation embedded in the deliverable, not a dispatch — PR hunts, Scout External Codebase clones.

substep.title: Compose the Register
substep.action:
Compose the register per references/research/document-format body-skeleton. Fixed top (what we sought precedent for, Summary) and bottom (Open Questions); the variable middle is the precedent entries grouped by transfer-fit class (directly-reusable first, then adaptable, informative, cautionary), followed by a Recommended Scout Follow-Ups subsection.
When no precedent was found in any source, the register says so plainly and cites the negative evidence ("codebase-pattern-finder: no similar implementation; thoughts-locator: no prior artifact; web: no documented pattern") — the greenfield finding.

check.template-output:
Present the register for review before persistence.
[a] Revise (re-hunt a source, sharpen an assessment) [c] Continue to persistence [y] YOLO
